case_title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSTION:$1,875.00 U.S. et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO FIRST SET OF DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS; PETITIONER'S FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES FILED BY PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA HEARING SET FOR OCT-03-2012 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-27-2013 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER COMPELLING ANSWERS TO FIRST SET OF DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF DOCUNMENTS; FIRST SET OF FORM INTERROGATORIES; FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES
STIPULATION TO JUDGMENT FILED BY PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA STABLES, MICHAEL R
POS OF ORDER DATED 10/3/12 FILED BY PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA

DISCOVERY 302, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO FIRST SET OF DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS; PETITIONER'S FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. WHILE THE MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION STATES THAT "[T]O DATE, NO RESPONSES HAVE BEEN RECEIVED FROM CLAIMANT," THAT IS NOT WHAT THE SUPPORTING DECLARATION SAYS. IN THE DECLARATION, COUNSEL REPRESENTS ONLY THAT "[T]O DATE, PETITIONER HAS NOT RECEIVED COMPLETE ANSWERS TO THESE DISCOVERY REQUESTS." DECLARATION OF PAMELA A. UNDERWOOD AT PARA. 3, EMPHASIS ADDED. BECAUSE THE SUBSTANTIVE AND PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS FOR MOTIONS COMPELLING RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY DIFFER, DEPENDING UPON WHETHER INADEQUATE ANSWERS WERE SERVED, OR NO ANSWERS AT ALL WERE PROVIDED, AND BECAUSE THE PAPERS SUBMITTED IN SUPPORT OF THIS MOTION ARE IN CONFLICT ON THIS ISSUE, THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. COURT TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: RICHARD GROTCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Counsel Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Feb-27-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: PAMELA UNDERWOOD, ASSISTANT DISTRICT ATTORNEY, OFFICE OF THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY, 415.551.9544, FOR PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA; NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Counsel Form Interrogatories IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM ERNST HALPERIN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Oct-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Unopposed Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner's First Set Of Special Interrogatories is DENIED without prejudice. While the memorandum in support of the motion states that "[t]o date, no responses have been received from Claimant," that is not what the supporting declaration says. In the declaration, counsel represents only that "[t]o date, Petitioner has not received complete answers to these discovery requests." Declaration of Pamela A. Underwood at para. 3, emphasis added. Because the substantive and procedural requirements for motions compelling responses to discovery differ, depending upon whether inadequate answers were served, or no answers at all were provided, and because the papers submitted in support of this motion are in conflict on this issue, the motion is DENIED without prejudice. Court to prepare order. Judge Pro Tem: Richard Grotch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported
